title: TM2T: Stochastic and Tokenized Modeling for the Reciprocal Generation of 3D Human Motions and Texts
abstract: Introduces the published HumanML3D M2T protocol.

title: MotionGPT: Human Motion as a Foreign Language
abstract: Reports M2T results under a different metric implementation.

title: G-Eval: NLG Evaluation using GPT-4 with Better Human Alignment
abstract: Uses rubric-driven chain-of-thought and form filling for NLG evaluation.

title: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
abstract: Establishes single-score and pairwise LLM judging and documents judge biases.

title: CLAIR: Evaluating Image Captions with Large Language Models
abstract: LLM scores candidate captions against references and explains the score.

title: Length-Controlled AlpacaEval: A Simple Way to Debias Automatic Evaluators
abstract: Reports length-controlled pairwise win rate to reduce verbosity bias.

title: From Crowdsourced Data to High-Quality Benchmarks: Arena-Hard and BenchBuilder Pipeline
abstract: Uses LLM pairwise judging and evaluates rank correlation and model separation.

title: Prometheus 2: An Open Source Language Model Specialized in Evaluating Other Language Models
abstract: Open judge supporting rubric-based direct assessment and pairwise ranking.

title: FLEUR: An Explainable Reference-Free Evaluation Metric for Image Captioning Using a Large Multimodal Model
abstract: Grounds caption evaluation in the source image and provides explanations.

title: JudgeBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-based Judges
abstract: Shows that strong judges can remain unreliable on hard preference pairs.

title: VideoJudge: Bootstrapping Enables Scalable Supervision of MLLM-as-a-Judge for Video Understanding
abstract: Finds video-conditioned judges outperform text-only judges for video-grounded responses.
